rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN STATE BROADCASTING, LLC VS. THOMAS CARTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Enrique Malvars Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Under Ccp 583.210
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 24, Part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling: DEFENDANT ENRIQUE MALVAR'S Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Under Ccp 583.210 Defendant Enrique Malvar's motion for mandatory dismissal under CCP 583.210 is granted. The only proof of service of the summons and complaint filed by plaintiff Golden State Broadcasting, LLC states that Mr. Malvar was substitute served at a UPS store on the last possible day to effect service to avoid the mandatory 3 year period for service. While not argued by GSB, substitute service on an individual is permitted at a UPS store without need for a showing that personal service could not be made after reasonable diligence per CCP 415.20(c). However, this form of substitute service is only permitted when "the only address reasonably known for the person to be served is a private mailbox." GSB cannot avail itself of this form or substitute service since the writ of possession it obtained against Mr. Malvar shows it knew of two addresses for Mr. Malvar other than the UPS store and there is no evidence that GSB had ruled out either, much less both, locations as places that Mr. Malvar could be personally served. Therefore, the only way that GSB could validly substitute serve Mr. Malvar was per CCP 415.20(b) which requires that reasonable diligence be shown that personal service cannot be made. The only showing that GSB has made that it tried to personally serve Mr. Malvar before its purported March 27, 2017 substitute service on him were four visits to the UPS store within four days of the last day for effecting service and two of those visits were when the UPS store was closed. Significantly, GBS has not shown that it made any effort to personally serve Mr. Malvar at either of the two addresses in the writ of possession. Evartt v. Superior Court (1979) 89 Cal. App. 3d 795 compels the conclusion that GSB failed to show the reasonable diligence to effect personal service before a party may resort to substitute service per CCP 415.20(b).

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN STATE BROADCASTING, LLC VS. THOMAS CARTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Enrique Malva'S Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Under Ccp Section 583.210
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 24, Part 2 of 2 of tentative ruling: Defendant Enrique Malvar'S Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Under Ccp Section 583.210 GSB's argument that Mr. Malvar's filing of an answer without filing a motion to quash bars Mr. Malvar from seeking dismissal for delay in prosecution per CCP 418.10(e)(3) lacks merit. While there is no case directly on point, analogous case law makes clear that, as stated in the subsection, the waiver effected by CCP 418.10(e)(3) only applies if a party files a demurrer or motion to strike and Mr. Malvar filed neither. (Britton v. Dallas Airmotive, Inc. (2007) 153 Cal. App. 4th 127, 133 (rejecting the argument that CCP 418.10(e)(3) provides for a waiver by filing an answer when the party had not previously filed a demurrer or motion to strike)). Moreover, case law prior to the enactment of CCP 418.10(e)(3) established that the filing of an answer more than 3 years after a complaint was filed did not constitute a waiver of the right to obtain dismissal on the grounds that service of summons and complaint had not been made within the mandatory 3 year period. (Biss v. Bohr (1995) 40 Cal. App. 4th 1246, 1251). GSB's effort to defeat this motion by relying on its unfiled proof of service also fails. Even if allowed to be filed (which should not be permitted because it is now more than 3 years and 60 days after the complaint was filed (CCP 583.220)), the unfiled proof of service does not satisfy the reasonable diligence requirement of CCP 415.20(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Malvar is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: GILBERTO ROBLES VASQUEZ ET AL VS. BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANTS BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA, INC., BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA II, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants Bosa Development California, Inc. and Bosa Development California II, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment on all claims alleged against them by plaintiff Gilberto Robles Vasquez complaint is granted as to both defendants. The undisputed facts show that Bosa CA did not direct the manner of the work performed by E&S and did not directly participate in that work. A "hirer's failure to correct an unsafe condition, by itself, does not establish an affirmative contribution." (Khosh v. Staples Construction Company, Inc. (2016) 4 Cal.App.5th 712, 718). Ray v. Silverado Constructors (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1120 is distinguishable because the general contractor in that case had exclusive control over the particular hazard, unlike here where control and responsibility for safety was shared as evidenced by the subcontract. There is no evidence that Bosa CA made a promise "to undertake a particular safety measure" or negligently failed to perform "that specific promise." (Khosh) Srithong v. Total Investment Co. (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 721 is inapposite because that case did not discuss the Privette doctrine as related to the liability of a hirer for injury to a worker employed by the contractor and focused on a third party leasing part of the owner's premises. The concealed hazard exception is not applicable because the improperly secured opening was not a pre-existing concealed hazard such that E&S could not have reasonably known about it through an inspection. The unsecured plywood covering the hole was marked "hole" with orange spray and there are no facts from which a reasonable trier of fact could infer that an inspection would not reasonably ascertain it. (Kinsman v. Unocal Corp. (2005) 37 Cal.4th 659, 677 ("the principles enunciated in Privette suggest that the landowner would not be liable when the contractor has failed to engage in inspections of the premises implicitly or explicitly delegated to it.")). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Bosa CA and Bosa II is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL D. WITT VS. FCA US LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And To Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL WITT'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And To Reopen Discovery. Plaintiff Michael Witt's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint and to reopen discovery is granted on specified conditions that Mr. Witt must accept in writing before the hearing or orally at the hearing and, if he does not, the motion is denied. The conditions on the granting of this motion are: 1) the trial is continued to March 4, 2019 at 9:30am in department 206 with all discovery deadlines triggered off the new trial date; 2) Mr. Witt must pay $10,000 to defendants FCA US LLC and Crown Automotive, Inc. no later than the date of entry of judgment in this case, which can be a setoff against any damages awarded in Mr. Witt's favor; and 3) Mr. Witt may not seek attorney's fees or costs against defendants for any time or costs incurred for this motion and preparation for the July 2, 2018 trial date. The conditions are necessary to eliminate the prejudice that defendants will suffer by the granting of the motion. $10,000 is a reasonable estimate for the fees and costs incurred by defendants to oppose this motion and to prepare for the July 2, 2018 trial date, which is properly borne by Mr. Witt, not defendants, due to Mr. Witt's lack of diligence in filing this motion. While Mr. Witt failed to show that he acted diligently in seeking to amend his complaint, if he accepts the conditions on the granting of the motion, California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings requires that the motion be granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Witt is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and recites whether Mr. Witt does or does not accept all of the conditions and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KATHLEEN OSWALD VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To The Third Cause Of Action For Breach Of Contract E: Failure To Defend And Fifth Cause Of Action For Declaratory Relief Re: Duty To Defend
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To The Third Cause Of Action For Breach Of Contract E: Failure To Defend And Fifth Cause Of Action For Declaratory Relief Re: Duty To Defend. Cross-complainant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary adjudication on its third and fifth causes of action for breach of contract and declaratory relief for a duty to defend against cross-defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator America is granted. Based on the undisputed facts, the City is entitled to an order that Thyssenkrupp is obligated to defend it against the claims alleged by plaintiff Kathleen Oswald in this case. Per the duty to defend language in the parties' contract and Crawford Weather Shield Manufacturing, Inc. (2008) 44 Cal. 44 541, Thyssenkrupp became obligated to defend the City against the claims alleged by Ms. Oswald in this case once the City made a demand that it be defended. All of Thyssenkrupp's procedural arguments lack merit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW BAILEY VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ANDREW BAILEY'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Stephen Zollman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in whole. Defendant to provide verified responses within thirty (30) days of the entry of this order. Parties are ordered to check in with the clerk, meet and confer and then notify the clerk upon completion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to szollman1@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 11, PLAINTIFFS BEATRIZ BULCAO, BRITNEY LOPEZ Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And Reopen Discovery. Plaintiffs Beatriz and Britney Lopez's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint and to reopen discovery is granted on specified conditions that plaintiffs must accept in writing before the hearing or orally at the hearing and, if they does not, the motion is denied. The conditions on the granting of this motion are: 1) the trial is continued to March 18, 2019 at 9:30am in department 206 with all discovery deadlines triggered off the new trial date; 2) plaintiffs must pay $3,000 to defendants FCA US LLC and Chrysler Jeep Dodge, Inc. no later than the date of entry of judgment in this case, which can be a setoff against any damages awarded in plaintiffs' favor; and 3) Plaintiffs may not seek attorney's fees or costs against defendants for any time or costs incurred for this motion and preparation for the September 17, 2018 trial date. The conditions are necessary to eliminate the prejudice that defendants will suffer by the granting of the motion. $3,000 is a reasonable estimate for the fees and costs incurred by defendants to oppose this motion and to prepare for the September 17, 2018 trial date, which are properly borne by plaintiffs, not defendants, due to plaintiffs' lack of diligence in filing this motion. While plaintiffs failed to show that he acted diligently in seeking to amend his complaint, if they accept the conditions on the granting of the motion, California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings requires that the motion be granted. rulings.Case Title: JOHN DG VS. DOE 1 ET AL

Defendants USA West Province, Society of Jesus and St. Ignatius Church's demurrer to the fifth, sixth and tenth causes for fraud, fiduciary/confidential relationship fraud and fraud and deceit in plaintiff's first amended complaint are sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff is given one last opportunity to attempt to plead with particularity one or more coherent fraud claims for intentional misrepresentation and/or concealment/partial disclosure. The fifth, sixth and tenth causes of action are currently a jumble of those two distinct fraud claims, improperly refer to a fiduciary relationship, and refer to misrepresentations which occurred after the alleged harm (sex abuse). rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GRICE VS. PRAKTEK CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 14, DEFENDANT EDWARD CUNNINGHAM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Edward Cunningham's demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Robert Grice is overruled. Jara v. Suprema Meats (2004) 121 Cal. App. 4th 1238, a decision of the First District which has not been overruled or criticized by any published authority, holds that a claim brought by a minority shareholder alleging that payments made by a corporation to a majority shareholder were in the form of excessive compensation to the majority shareholder and can be the basis of an individual claim on behalf of the minority shareholder against the majority shareholder. Liberally construed, the third cause of action alleges that Praktek Corporation made payments to Mr. Cunningham or entities controlled by Mr. Cunningham in the form of excessive compensation to Mr. Cunningham and thus Jara permits Mr. Grice to sue on his own behalf based on those payments. The fact that there are two other shareholders in Praktek who hold a small percentage interest in the company does not warrant a different result under the reasoning in Jara. The two cases primarily relied on by Mr. Cunningham predate Jara and are distinguished in Jara as involving claims "dealing with mismanagement ¿ or fraud." The third cause of action does not allege either mismanagement or fraud by Mr. Cunningham, so those cases (Nelson and PacLink) are inapplicable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Grice is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNT OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MICHAEL PATRICK MORAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 16, DEFENDANT MICHAEL MORAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Michael Moran's demurrer to both causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff City and County of San Francisco is overruled as to both causes of action. Per common parlance and understanding as well as the Code of Civil Procedure, a lien is not a claim in a lawsuit and thus the filing of a lien by the City in Mr. Moran's lawsuit against Yellow Cab does not constitute "another action" for purposes of a demurrer based on another action pending. Medical lien law makes clear that a medical provider's assertion of a lien in a personal injury lawsuit does not preclude the medical provider from asserting other remedies to recover for services it provided. Mr. Moran's recourse, if any, to avoid what he considers duplicative proceedings is to seek a discretionary stay of this action until the Yellow Cab case is resolved. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TRENT JASON VS. AMERICAN AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION OF NORTHERN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 17, DEFENDANT VALERA BARNHART MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Valera Barnhart's motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint is granted as to all irrelevant allegations sought to be stricken alleged in the first amended complaint by plaintiff Trent Jason with twenty days leave to amend. Mr. Jason has twenty days to file a succinct, no more than thirty page second amended complaint that removes all legal arguments and only alleges, without repetition, the ultimate facts supporting the elements of each his claims. (CCP 425.10(a) ("A complaint ¿ shall contain ¿ A statement of facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language")). If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Jason also has leave to allege conduct that supports the imposition of punitive damages against Ms. Barnhart. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Ms. Barnhart is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TRENT JASON VS. AMERICAN AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION OF NORTHERN
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 17, DEFENDANT VALERA BARNHART DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Valera Barnhart's demurrer to the seventh and ninth causes of action for fraud and negligent infliction of emotional distress in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Trent Jason is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to both causes of action. Mr. Jason has not adequately alleged that he justifiably relied on Ms. Barnhart's alleged fraud or that the alleged fraud resulted in damages. He has also not adequately alleged any facts to support his NIED claim against Ms. Barnhart. Mr. Jason has twenty days to file a succinct, no more than thirty page second amended complaint that removes all legal arguments and only alleges, without repetition, the ultimate facts supporting the elements of each his claims. (CCP 425.10(a) ("A complaint ¿ shall contain ¿ A statement of facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language")). rulings.Case Title: TRENT JASON VS. AMERICAN AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION OF NORTHERN
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 17, DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE, STEPHEN PERRANDO, CSAA INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants CSAA Insurance Services, Inc. CSAA Insurance Exchange, and Stephen Perrando's demurrer to the all causes of action alleged against them in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Trent Jason is: a) overruled as to the first, second, and third causes of action for breach of contract, breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and insurance bad faith for failure to properly investigate a claim; b) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth through seventh and ninth causes of action for unfair competition, misrepresentation, false promise, fraud, and negligent infliction of emotion distress; and c) sustained without leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action for unlawful practice of law. Defendants' arguments based on contractual interpretation are not amenable to resolution at the pleading stage. The facts alleged show that Perrando did not engage in unlawful practice of law and no reasonable person would believe that his letter constituted legal advice to Mr. Jason. Mr. Jason has twenty days to file a succinct, no more than thirty page second amended complaint that removes all legal arguments and only alleges, without repetition, the ultimate facts supporting the elements of each his claims. (CCP 425.10(a) ("A complaint ¿ shall contain ¿ A statement of facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language")). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for demurring defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DEMAS YAN VS. BRYANT FU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Vexatious Litigant Demas Yan To Post Security
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2018, Line 18, DEFENDANTS BRYANT FU, CRYSTAL LEI'S d Motion For An Order Requiring Vexatious Litigant Demas Yan To Post Security. Defendants Crystal Lei and Bryant Fu's motion for an order requiring vexatious litigant Demas Yan to post security is granted. Per CCP 391.3 and 391.4, unless Mr. Yan furnishes acceptable security in the amount of $50,000 for the benefit of Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu no later than July 31, 2018, this case will be dismissed as to Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu on August 1, 2018. Per CCP 391.6, this case is stayed until July 31, 2018 or the date as Mr. Yan furnishes the required security, whichever occurs first. Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu have shown that Mr. Yan is a vexatious litigant within the meaning of CCP 391(b) and that there is no reasonable possibility that Mr. Yan will prevail in this case. $50,000 is the minimum reasonable amount that Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu are likely to incur to defend themselves in this case. Although Mr. Yan is represented by an attorney in this case, that representation does not preclude Mr. Yan from the posting security requirement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
